frameworks.name: fronto-parietal vs MTL memory-systems dichotomy
frameworks.one_line: Traditional view separating STM from LTM is challenged by hippocampal STM deficits

frameworks.name: thalamo-cortical connectivity
frameworks.one_line: Thalamic nuclei regulate cortico-cortical interactions supporting memory

frameworks.name: hippocampal contribution to working memory
frameworks.one_line: Direct hippocampal-thalamic anatomy mediates STM beyond LTM

core_question: Does thalamic damage linked to hippocampal dysfunction contribute to short-term memory deficits in MTL epilepsy patients?
key_assumptions: Hippocampus has direct anatomical thalamic connections
Thalamic subdivisions can be identified via diffusion-weighted tractography
Resting-state functional connectivity reflects functionally relevant circuits
manipulations: Patient group vs control (MTL epilepsy patients with hippocampal dysfunction)
Cortical target region (frontal, temporal, parietal divisions)
key_findings: Thalamo-temporal functional connectivity tracked LTM performance
Thalamo-prefrontal functional connectivity specifically predicted STM performance
Patients with hippocampal volume loss also showed thalamic volume loss, prominent in pulvinar
Behavioural association with STM was lost in the epileptic hemisphere
interpretations: Thalamic disruption secondary to hippocampal damage contributes to STM deficits
STM and LTM rely on dissociable thalamo-cortical loops rather than purely separate cortical systems
